AICompletionResult.description: Schema for AI chat completion results.

AIImageResult.description: Schema for an AI image generation result.

id.description: Tool call ID this result responds to

Acl.description:
Reusable API schema for access control lists.

Supports two modes (mutually exclusive):

**Replace mode** — send `grants` to replace all entries:

    {"grants": [{"principal_type": "user", "principal": "...", "actions": ["read"]}]}

Use `{"grants": []}` to clear all entries.

**Patch mode** — send `add` and/or `remove`:

    {"add": [...grants...], "remove": [{"principal_type": "user", "principal": "..."}]}

Cannot mix `grants` with `add`/`remove`.

add.description: Patch mode: grants to add or merge into existing

grants.description: Replace mode: full list of grants (replaces all existing). Use [] to clear.

remove.description: Patch mode: principals to remove from existing

AclGrant.description: API schema for a single ACL grant entry.

actions.description: List of allowed actions (e.g. read, write)

principal.description: Principal identifier (UUID for user/team/org/agent_user, role name for org_role, omit for everyone)

principal_type.description: Principal type: user, team, org, org_role, agent, or everyone

AclRemoveTarget.description: API schema for identifying a principal to remove from an ACL.

principal.description: Principal identifier to remove (omit for everyone)

principal_type.description: Principal type to remove

Actor.description:
Schema for a message actor (user or agent).

Actors represent the entity that sent a message.
Maps to the actor format from MessageActorHelper.build_actor/1.

acl.description: Access control list. Contains grants array with principal_type, principal, and actions. Null when no ACL restrictions are applied.

AgentComputer.description: API schema for an agent computer.

AgentExport.description:
API schema for an agent export response.

Contains the reconstructed AgentTemplate and all dependent config files
needed for a fully self-contained re-deploy.

AgentRoutine.description: API schema for an agent routine.

acl.description: Access control list. Contains grants array with principal_type, principal, and actions. Null when no ACL restrictions are applied.

steps.description: Ordered list of chain steps (present when handler_type is "chain"). Each step is a plain map with handler_type, optional body fields (preset_name / preset_config / script / config), and step-local plumbing (name, inputs, output_key, on_error).

AgentRoutineRun.description: API schema for an agent routine run.

acl.description: Access control list. Contains grants array with principal_type, principal, and actions. Null when no ACL restrictions are applied.

structured_response.description: Validated structured response when the run uses an AgentMessageSchema

worker.description: Background worker status. Null when no worker job is associated.

AgentRoutineRunListResponse.description: Cursor-paginated list response for agent routine runs.

after_cursor.description: Cursor for fetching items after this point

before_cursor.description: Cursor for fetching items before this point

AgentSchedule.description: API schema for an agent schedule.

AgentSession.description: API schema for an agent session.

is_system_session.description: Whether this is a system-created session

name.description: Optional display name for the session

status.description: Session status (pending, running, waiting, completed, failed, cancelled)

trajectory.description: Trajectory ID for the durable session transcript

AgentSkill.description: API schema for an agent skill.

AgentSkillList.description: API schema for agent skills list response.

AgentTool.description: API schema for an agent tool.

Attachment.description:
Schema for a message attachment.

Attachments can be of various types (file, scraped_link, artifact, task, media, action).
Fields present depend on the attachment type.
Maps to format_attachments_for_client/1 output.

content_type.description: MIME content type (file, artifact, media types)

image_source.description: Image metadata (file, scraped_link, artifact, media types)

type.description: Attachment type: file, scraped_link, artifact, task, media, action

url.description: URL to the resource (file, scraped_link, artifact, media types)

AuthTokens.description: API schema for authentication token responses.

AutomationRun.description:
Schema for an automation run.

Maps to serialized automation run output from developer portal API.

status.description: Status: pending, running, completed, failed, cancelled

BuiltinTool.description: An individual tool within a builtin tool catalog entry.

BuiltinToolCatalogEntry.description: A builtin tool catalog entry describing an available tool category.

ComputerExecResult.description: Result of executing a command on an agent computer.

Config.description: API schema for a config resource.

relative_path.description: Path within parent bundle (bundle children only)

ConfigKindSchema.description: Schema for a config kind's JSON schema and sample response.

json_schema.description: JSON Schema for this config kind (can be null if not available)

sample_yaml.description: Sample YAML content for this config kind (can be null if not available)

ConfigVersion.description: API schema for a config version.

CustomObject.description: API schema for a custom object.

DeviceAuthorizationResponse.description: API schema for OAuth device authorization responses.

DeviceAuthorizationStatusResponse.description: API schema for OAuth device authorization approval and denial responses.

ImageSource.description: API schema for image source metadata.

InstallationKind.description: API schema for an installation kind.

requires_integration.description: Whether this kind requires an integration

InstallationSource.description: API schema for an installation source.

InviteCreator.description:
Minimal public-safe projection of the user who created an invite.

Invites are fetched by unauthenticated recipients on the `/auth/invites/:id`
route, so this schema deliberately exposes only the fields needed to render a
join screen — id, display name, and profile picture. Email, org, sandbox, and
other fields on the full `User` schema are omitted.

KeyValueStorageEntry.description:
Schema for a key-value storage entry.

Maps exactly to render_entry/1 output in ApiStorageController.

KeyValueStorageEntryPage.description:
Response envelope for the dual-mode key-value list endpoint.

User-JWT callers receive `%{data: [...]}` (no pagination fields).
Developer / S2S callers receive `%{data: [...]}` plus pagination metadata.

has_next.description: Whether a next page exists (developer / S2S only)

has_prev.description: Whether a previous page exists (developer / S2S only)

total_entries.description: Total entries matching the filters (developer / S2S only)

total_pages.description: Total number of pages (developer / S2S only)

KeyValueStorageEntryWithUser.description:
Schema for a key-value storage entry, optionally enriched with a summary of
the owning user.

Used by the dual-mode list endpoint: user-JWT callers omit the user_email /
user_name fields (they know who they are); developer / S2S callers populate
them so the portal can render an owner column without a per-row lookup.

LLMConfig.description:
API schema for per-routine LLM invocation settings.

Mirrors `ArchAstro.Agents.Routines.LLMConfig`. When `model` is present,
it overrides the agent's `default_model` for the routine (or step).

model.description: Model identifier. When set, overrides the agent's default_model.

MediaVariant.description: API schema for a media variant.

Message.description: API schema for a chat message.

agent.description: Agent if sent by an agent user

branched_thread.description: Branched thread (if message spawned a thread)

legacy_agent.description: Legacy agent if sent by legacy chat agent

user.description: Author user (public ID or expanded object when loaded)

MessageReaction.description:
Schema for inline message reactions.

This is the compact format used in Message.reactions[], which differs from
the full Reaction schema used in standalone reaction endpoints.
Maps to format_reactions_for_client/1 output.

OAuthTokenResponse.description: API schema for OAuth token endpoint responses.

PaginatedReplies.description:
Schema for paginated message replies response.

Used by message replies list endpoints.
Note: This response is NOT wrapped in a "data" field.

after_cursor.description: Cursor for fetching items after this point

before_cursor.description: Cursor for fetching items before this point

has_more.description: Whether more replies exist beyond the current page

PresetConfig.description:
API schema for preset handler configuration.

Mirrors `ArchAstro.Agents.Routines.PresetConfig`. Used wherever a request
or response carries a preset config — routine-level and chain-step-level.

Server-side validation (length/format rules on `instructions`, enum checks
on `session_mode`/`session_scope`, model lookup against
`ChatCompletion.models/0`) is authoritative. This schema defines the
request shape and typed response shape so OpenAPI consumers see real
types instead of an opaque object.

instructions.description: Custom task or behavior instructions for the preset (max 10,000 chars).

llm.description: LLM invocation settings (e.g. a `model` override for this routine/step).

session_mode.description: Session mode: `stateless` (default, new session per trigger) or `session` (find-or-create a persistent session scoped by `session_scope`).

session_scope.description: When `session_mode` is `session`, controls session scoping: `per_user` (default), `per_key`, `per_org`, or `global`.

structured_message_template_ids.description: Config IDs of AgentMessageSchema templates that constrain the agent's responses to predefined structured formats.

RoutinePreset.description:
A routine preset entry in the preset listing endpoint. Describes *what*
a preset is, not a per-routine configuration — that's `PresetConfig`.

applicable_events.description: Event types this preset accepts. `["*"]` means any event. Other event types are rejected at routine validation time.

chainable.description: Whether this preset may appear as a step inside a `:chain` routine. Presets with session or async execution models are not chainable.

sessionable.description: Whether this preset maintains a persistent chatroom session across turns (e.g. `participate`). Sessionable presets don't expose instructions or session-mode fields at the routine level.

unique.description: Whether only one routine with this preset may exist per agent. Enforced at the AgentRoutine unique-index layer.

acl.description: Access control list. Contains grants array with principal_type, principal, and actions. Null when no ACL restrictions are applied.

membership_status.description: Viewer's membership role (owner, admin, member) or null if not a member

TeamInvite.description: Schema for a team invite response.

TeamMembership.description: API schema for a team membership.

TeamMembershipListResponse.description: Paginated list response for team memberships.

Thread.description: API schema for a chat thread.

is_default.description: Whether this is the default thread

ThreadMember.description: API schema for a thread member.

ThreadReadStatus.description: Schema for thread read status response.

ThreadSettings.description:
Schema for thread settings response.

Used by thread settings show/update endpoints.

agent_enabled.description: Whether the agent is enabled for this thread

UserInvite.description: API schema for a user invite.

WorkerStatus.description: API schema for background worker status on a routine run.

attempt.description: Current attempt number (0 = not yet attempted)

status.description: Worker state: queued, executing, retrying, completed, discarded, or cancelled

WorkingMemoryEntry.description: API schema for a working memory entry.

WorkingMemoryEntryListResponse.description: Paginated list response for working memory entries.

event_config.description: Event config map. Keys are event types, values are entry objects with "filters" (filter map) and optional "dedupe_key_path" (JSON path for deduplication, e.g. "$.thread.id")

handler_type.description: Handler type: workflow_graph, script, preset, or chain

instructions.description: Custom task or behavior instructions for the preset (max 10,000 chars).

llm.description: LLM invocation settings (e.g. a `model` override for this routine/step).

model.description: Model identifier. When set, overrides the agent's default_model.

session_mode.description: Session mode: `stateless` (default, new session per trigger) or `session` (find-or-create a persistent session scoped by `session_scope`).

session_scope.description: When `session_mode` is `session`, controls session scoping: `per_user` (default), `per_key`, `per_org`, or `global`.

structured_message_template_ids.description: Config IDs of AgentMessageSchema templates that constrain the agent's responses to predefined structured formats.

steps.description: Linear sequence of steps (required when handler_type is "chain"; must be empty otherwise). See the Step schema for per-step fields. Replaces the step list entirely — send the full desired list, not a diff.

items.description:
API schema for a single chain-routine step.

Mirrors the shape of `ArchAstro.Agents.Routines.Step` as seen by clients,
with one API-side normalization: clients send `config` (matching the
routine-level field name), and `cast/1` rewrites it to `config_id` so
downstream changeset code sees the canonical key.

Server-side validation (handler-body mutual exclusion, `config_id`
existence + kind, unique step names within a chain) is authoritative
— this schema only defines the *shape* of the request payload.

config.description: Config ID (for handler_type: workflow_graph, or as an alternative to inline `script` when handler_type: script). Normalized to `config_id` internally.

handler_type.description: Handler type for this step: preset, script, or workflow_graph

inputs.description: Optional map binding prior step outputs to this step's inputs.

name.description: Optional step label. Must be unique within the chain if set.

on_error.description: Error policy: halt (default), continue, or retry.

output_key.description: Optional name under which this step's result is addressable downstream. Defaults to `name`.

preset_config.description: Preset configuration (same shape as the routine-level preset_config). May include `llm: {model}` to override the agent's default_model for this step.

instructions.description: Custom task or behavior instructions for the preset (max 10,000 chars).

llm.description: LLM invocation settings (e.g. a `model` override for this routine/step).

model.description: Model identifier. When set, overrides the agent's default_model.

session_mode.description: Session mode: `stateless` (default, new session per trigger) or `session` (find-or-create a persistent session scoped by `session_scope`).

session_scope.description: When `session_mode` is `session`, controls session scoping: `per_user` (default), `per_key`, `per_org`, or `global`.

structured_message_template_ids.description: Config IDs of AgentMessageSchema templates that constrain the agent's responses to predefined structured formats.

422.description: Unprocessable entity - no workflow config attached

session_key.description: Session key (required when session_scope is per_key)

user.description: User ID (S2S/developer only; client uses viewer)

Reconstructs an AgentTemplate config from a deployed agent and its sub-resources
(tools, routines, skills, installations). Returns the template plus all dependent
config files (scripts, workflows, skills, schemas) with their raw content for a
fully self-contained export.

items.description:
Public org schema for authenticated endpoints.

Only exposes fields safe for any authenticated user: id, name, domain,
and logo. Does NOT expose sandbox, status, industry, description, or
other internal fields that the Developer.Org schema includes.

403.description: App-scoped token required. Use a token scoped to the target app.

post.description:
Accepts either `join_code` or `invite_code`.

For user-authenticated requests, the current user joins the team identified by
the invite code. For server-to-server requests, provide `agent` or `user` to
add that principal to the team instead.

agent.description: Optional agent ID — when provided, adds the agent to the team instead of the user

user.description: User ID to join (required for S2S requests without agent_id)

post.summary: Join a team using an invite code

post.description:
Joins a specific visible team by team ID.

For standard user requests, the current user is added to the team. When
`agent` is provided, the current user must already be a team member and the
agent is added instead.

agent.description: Optional agent ID — when provided, adds the agent to the team instead of the user

post.summary: Join a team the current user can see

x-channels.description:
Channel for real-time chat messaging.

Supports team-scoped and user-scoped threads with keyed, transient, and direct
thread access patterns.

joins.description: Join a team-scoped thread by ID
joins.name: join_team_thread

joins.description: Join or create a team-scoped keyed thread
joins.name: join_team_keyed

joins.description: Join a user-scoped thread by ID
joins.name: join_user_thread

joins.description: Join or create a user-scoped keyed thread
joins.name: join_user_keyed

messages.description: Fork a sub-thread from an existing message
Mark a thread as read up to a given message
List all messages in the current thread
Load additional messages with cursor-based pagination
Post a new message with optional uploads and reply-to
Add an emoji reaction to a message
Remove an emoji reaction from a message

x-channels.name: ApiChatChannel

pushes.description: Broadcast when a new message is added to a thread

x-channels.description:
Phoenix channel for real-time activity feed updates.

Clients join a topic scoped to an agent or org and receive
`new_entry` events as feed entries are created.

## Topics

  * `"api:activity_feed:agent:{agent_user_id}"` — entries for a specific agent
  * `"api:activity_feed:org:{org_id}"` — entries for an entire org/tenant

messages.description: List activity feed entries with cursor-based pagination

x-channels.name: ApiActivityFeedChannel

x-channels.description:
Channel for real-time custom object collaboration.

Clients join `api:object:{object_id}` to receive the current object state
and subscribe to field-level updates. Mutations are sent as key:value maps.
x-channels.name: ApiObjectChannel

login.description: Create a client by logging in with email/password
